To: Compliance Committee; From: Corporate Legal, Velispring Therapeutics, Inc.; CC: investorrelations@velispring.example
Subject: US federal securities disclosures (Reg FD) and data privacy controls in current outreach
Background: The Q1 results, pipeline update, and liquidity discussion were publicly furnished via Form 8-K and the Form 10-Q filed 04/30, with the webcast replay and slide deck dated 05/05 available on our website.
NEOM listing notice dated 05/01 regarding earnings pre-announcement practices is public; no action is required beyond confirming our disclosure calendar.
FHPA meeting schedule for VSP-201 trial (press release 03/15) is stale public information; internal references to that schedule are not MNPI.
Vendor onboarding at Northbridge Community Bank for payroll and investigator payments is an operational update; the generic “Account Ref: 0000-XX-VOID” is not a customer account number and is not PII or MNPI.
Remind teams that de-identified patient metrics must comply with HIPAA safe-harbor and GLBA exclusions; no patient names, addresses, or direct identifiers appear in the analyst deck.
The prior annual training covered insider trading and Rule 10b-5 concepts in general; this memo does not allege misconduct.
Intentional sensitivity: On 06/10, during a one-on-one diligence call, our CFO informed an Aldercrest Research analyst that Q2 net patient enrollments in VSP-201 are running ahead of previously disclosed guidance; until broadly disclosed, this statement is material non-public information under Reg FD.
Recommended cure: disseminate a press release and furnish a Form 8-K within 24 hours using the standard “Item 7.01/2.02” template, and ensure all subsequent investor conversations reference only publicly posted materials.
Pre-clearance: employees must route any prospective quantitative updates through IR; avoid selective disclosure during non-deal roadshows and observe quarterly blackout windows as posted on the intranet.
Note that the external “Broad Street Chronicle” article dated 04/10 about manufacturing capacity is speculative media and does not convert speculation into issuer disclosure.
For completeness, the USCMA’s Staff Bulletin on Market Communications (issued 01/12) is cited in our policy manual; it is a public document and may be shared.
Questions may be directed to compliance@velispring.example or the public helpline at 1-800-000-0000; do not include any patient-level data or customer financial details in correspondence.
